mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near zero and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, markets infer prolonged accommodation and higher future inflation risk; that combination pushes the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days as real-rate expectations and term premium compress.
failure_condition: Rejected if 10-year yields rise over the next 20 trading days in most periods where FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL remains above its recent trend, or if the sign of the yield response is positive for two consecutive triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A further rise in UNRATE above a recent elevated level signals weakening labor demand and a growth slowdown, which lowers expected policy rates and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if yield changes are nonnegative in a majority of episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, or if the 20-day forward yield response is not lower than baseline after unemployment shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals resilient household spending and labor income, which supports stronger nominal demand and lifts Treasury term premium and expected policy rates, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if combined PAYEMS and RSAFS improvements do not coincide with positive 20-day yield moves more often than not, or if the sign relationship disappears in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates after a weak patch, firms are reactivating supply capacity and commodity-sensitive growth expectations firm, which raises real-yield expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day yield direction is not positive following monthly INDPRO gains above 0.5%, or if the effect reverses in successive rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL surprise above a still-low FEDFUNDS rate forces markets to reprice future tightening, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days through a higher expected policy path and a steeper real-rate curve.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation acceleration relative to the policy rate fails to produce higher 20-day yields, or if the relationship is absent when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the contemporaneous FEDFUNDS level by an expanding margin.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market stress regime, an improving PAYEMS print while UNRATE stays high indicates stabilization rather than overheating, which reduces recession fears and lifts the 10-year Treasury yield modestly over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if yield direction is not positive in high-unemployment periods with improving PAYEMS, or if the sign flips negative despite persistent stabilization in labor data.

mechanism_narrative: After a housing downturn, a rebound in HOUST transmits to broader cyclical demand and higher growth expectations, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month housing starts recoveries do not precede higher yields over the next month, or if the effect is absent in repeated housing rebound episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary industrial slowdown, falling INDPRO together with weak RSAFS signals demand destruction and lower inflation pressure, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous declines in INDPRO and RSAFS are not followed by lower 20-day yields, or if the relationship fails across rolling windows with repeated industrial contractions.
